import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Fetch the content of the specified URL with proper SSL context
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context) as response:
            # Get encoding from Content-Type header, fall back to UTF-8
            content_type = response.getheader('Content-Type', '')
            encoding = 'utf-8'
            if 'charset=' in content_type:
                encoding = content_type.split('charset=')[-1].strip().split(';')[0].strip()
            
            # Read and decode content with fallback for encoding errors
            raw_content = response.read()
            try:
                content = raw_content.decode(encoding)
            except UnicodeDecodeError:
                content = raw_content.decode('utf-8', errors='replace')

        # Extract title (case-insensitive, handles multi-line tags)
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description (handles any attribute order, quote styles)
        desc_match = re.search(
            r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']|'
            r'<meta[^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\'][^>]*name=["\']description["\']',
            content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL
        )
        if desc_match:
            description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match.group(1) else desc_match.group(2).strip()
        else:
            description = "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, ValueError, ConnectionError):
        # Handle all common fetch/parse errors with default values
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:

        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}

        # Validate initial URL scheme and hostname
        parsed_url = urlparse(page_url)
        if parsed_url.scheme not in ('http', 'https'):
            raise ValueError("Only HTTP/HTTPS URLs are allowed")
        
        hostname = parsed_url.hostname
        if not hostname:
            raise ValueError("Invalid URL: missing hostname")

        # Block access to private/loopback/link-local addresses
        def is_restricted_address(host: str) -> bool:
            """Check if hostname resolves to any restricted IP address."""
            try:
                addrs = socket.getaddrinfo(host, None, socket.AF_UNSPEC, socket.SOCK_STREAM)
                for addr in addrs:
                    ip_str = addr[4][0]
                    ip_obj = ipaddress.ip_address(ip_str)
                    if ip_obj.is_private or ip_obj.is_loopback or ip_obj.is_link_local:
                        return True
                return False
            except (socket.gaierror, ValueError):

        if is_restricted_address(hostname):
            raise ValueError("Access to restricted addresses is prohibited")

        # Custom redirect handler to validate all redirect targets
        class SafeRedirectHandler(urllib.request.HTTPRedirectHandler):
            def redirect_request(self, req, fp, code, msg, headers, newurl):
                parsed_new = urlparse(newurl)
                if parsed_new.scheme not in ('http', 'https'):
                    return None
                
                new_host = parsed_new.hostname
                if not new_host or is_restricted_address(new_host):
                    return None
                
                return super().redirect_request(req, fp, code, msg, headers, newurl)

        # Setup secure context and opener

        return {

            'description': description

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
import ssl
import certifi
from html.parser import HTMLParser


class MetaDataParser(HTMLParser):
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.title = ""
        self.description = ""
        self._in_title_tag = False
        self._title_collected = False

    def handle_starttag(self, tag, attrs):
        tag_lower = tag.lower()
        if tag_lower == "title":
            self._in_title_tag = True
            self._title_collected = False
        elif tag_lower == "meta":
            attr_dict = dict(attrs)
            # Extract standard meta description
            if "name" in attr_dict and attr_dict["name"].lower() == "description":
                if not self.description:
                    self.description = attr_dict.get("content", "").strip()
            # Extract Open Graph description (common in social media)
            elif "property" in attr_dict and attr_dict["property"].lower() == "og:description":
                if not self.description:
                    self.description = attr_dict.get("content", "").strip()
            # Extract Twitter Card description
            elif "name" in attr_dict and attr_dict["name"].lower() == "twitter:description":
                if not self.description:
                    self.description = attr_dict.get("content", "").strip()

    def handle_data(self, data):
        if self._in_title_tag and not self._title_collected:
            self.title += data.strip()

    def handle_endtag(self, tag):
        if tag.lower() == "title":
            self._in_title_tag = False
            self._title_collected = True


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches title and description metadata from a given webpage URL.
    
    Args:
        page_url: The full URL of the webpage to scrape (must include scheme like https://).
        
    Returns:
        Dictionary containing:
            - 'title': Extracted page title (empty string if not found)
            - 'description': Extracted page description (empty string if not found)
            - 'error': Optional error message (only present if an issue occurred)
    """
    # Create secure SSL context using certifi's trusted CA bundle
    ssl_context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
    
    result = {
        "title": "",
        "description": ""
    }
    
    try:
        # Fetch webpage with timeout to prevent hanging connections
        with urllib.request.urlopen(
            page_url, 
            context=ssl_context, 
            timeout=10
        ) as response:
            # Determine content encoding (fallback to UTF-8 if not specified)
            content_charset = response.headers.get_content_charset() or "utf-8"
            # Decode content safely, replacing invalid bytes
            html_content = response.read().decode(content_charset, errors="replace")
            
            # Parse HTML to extract metadata
            parser = MetaDataParser()
            parser.feed(html_content)
            parser.close()
            
            # Update result with parsed metadata
            result["title"] = parser.title
            result["description"] = parser.description
            
    except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
        result["error"] = f"HTTP error: {e.code} - {e.reason}"
    except urllib.error.URLError as e:
        result["error"] = f"Network/URL error: {str(e.reason)}"
    except ValueError as e:
        result["error"] = f"Invalid URL: {str(e)}"
    except Exception as e:
        result["error"] = f"Unexpected error: {str(e)}"
    
    return result

import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import Dict, Any

import ipaddress
import http.client


def is_internal_hostname(hostname: str) -> bool:
    """Check if a hostname resolves to an internal/private IP address."""
    try:
        # Resolve hostname to all possible IP addresses (IPv4 and IPv6)
        addrs = socket.getaddrinfo(hostname, None, socket.AF_UNSPEC, socket.SOCK_STREAM)
        for addr in addrs:
            ip_str = addr[4][0]
            try:
                ip_obj = ipaddress.ip_address(ip_str)
                
                # Check for IPv4-mapped IPv6 addresses
                if isinstance(ip_obj, ipaddress.IPv6Address) and ip_obj.ipv4_mapped:
                    ipv4_obj = ip_obj.ipv4_mapped
                    if ipv4_obj.is_private or ipv4_obj.is_loopback or ipv4_obj.is_link_local or ipv4_obj.is_reserved:
                        return True
                
                # Check standard internal/loopback/reserved ranges
                if ip_obj.is_private or ip_obj.is_loopback or ip_obj.is_link_local or ip_obj.is_reserved:
                    return True
                    
            except ValueError:
                # Skip invalid IP addresses
                continue
        return False
    except socket.gaierror:
        # Hostname resolution failed - treat as non-internal (will fail later in fetch)
        return False


class SSRFSafeRedirectHandler(urllib.request.HTTPRedirectHandler):
    """Custom redirect handler to block redirects to internal hosts."""
    def redirect_request(self, req, fp, code, msg, headers, new_url):
        parsed_new_url = urlparse(new_url)
        
        # Block non-HTTP/HTTPS redirects
        if parsed_new_url.scheme not in ('http', 'https') or not parsed_new_url.netloc:
            return None
            
        # Block redirects to internal hosts
        if is_internal_hostname(parsed_new_url.hostname):
            return None
            
        # Allow valid redirects
        return super().redirect_request(req, fp, code, msg, headers, new_url)


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    # Validate URL scheme and structure
    parsed_url = urlparse(page_url)
    if parsed_url.scheme not in ('http', 'https') or not parsed_url.netloc:
        return {
            'title': 'Invalid URL',
            'description': 'URL must be a valid HTTP or HTTPS address with a domain name.'
        }

    # Block access to internal/private hosts
    if is_internal_hostname(parsed_url.hostname):
        return {
            'title': 'Forbidden URL',
            'description': 'Access to internal or private network addresses is not allowed.'
        }

    try:
        # Set up SSL context with trusted certificates
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        
        # Create SSRF-safe opener with redirect handling and proper SSL context
        opener = urllib.request.build_opener(
            SSRFSafeRedirectHandler(),
            urllib.request.HTTPSHandler(context=context),
            urllib.request.HTTPHandler()
        )
        # Set a valid user agent to avoid being blocked by servers
        opener.addheaders = [
            ('User-Agent', 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/118.0.0.0 Safari/537.36')
        ]

        # Fetch page content with timeout to prevent hanging
        with opener.open(page_url, timeout=10) as response:
            # Parse content encoding from Content-Type header
            content_type = response.getheader('Content-Type', '')
            main_type, params = http.client.parse_header(content_type)
            encoding = params.get('charset', 'utf-8')
            
            # Read and decode content with error handling
            content = response.read().decode(encoding, errors='replace')

        # Extract and normalize title (handles tags with attributes)
        title_match = re.search(r'<title[^>]*>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        if title_match:
            title_text = title_match.group(1).strip()
            title = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', title_text)
        else:
            title = "No title found"

        # Extract and normalize meta description (handles attribute order and escaped quotes)
        description = "No description available"
        # First find any meta tag with name="description" (case-insensitive, any attribute order)
        meta_desc_tags = re.findall(r'<meta[^>]*>', content, re.IGNORECASE)
        for tag in meta_desc_tags:
            if re.search(r'name=["\']description["\']', tag, re.IGNORECASE):
                # Extract content value with support for escaped quotes
                desc_match = re.search(r'content=["\']([^"\\]*(?:\\.[^"\\]*)*)["\']', tag, re.IGNORECASE)
                if desc_match:
                    desc_text = desc_match.group(1).strip()
                    # Unescape quoted characters
                    desc_text = re.sub(r'\\(["\'])', r'\1', desc_text)
                    # Normalize whitespace
                    description = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', desc_text)
                    break

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, socket.timeout):
        # Handle network errors, HTTP errors, and timeouts
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content due to network issues, server errors, or timeout.'
        }

from urllib.parse import urlparse

from ipaddress import ip_address, AddressValueError


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Parse and validate URL structure
        parsed_url = urlparse(page_url)

        # Restrict to HTTP/HTTPS schemes only
        if parsed_url.scheme not in ('http', 'https'):
            raise ValueError("Only HTTP and HTTPS URLs are allowed.")

        # Extract and validate hostname
        hostname = parsed_url.netloc.split(':')[0]
        if not hostname:
            raise ValueError("Invalid URL: missing hostname.")

        # Resolve hostname to IP addresses
        try:
            ips = socket.gethostbyname_ex(hostname)[2]
        except socket.gaierror as e:
            raise ValueError(f"Failed to resolve hostname: {str(e)}") from e

        # Block access to internal network addresses
        for ip in ips:
            try:
                addr = ip_address(ip)
                if addr.is_private or addr.is_loopback or addr.is_link_local:
                    raise ValueError("Access to internal network addresses is prohibited.")
            except AddressValueError:
                continue  # Skip invalid IP addresses

        # Fetch content with timeout to prevent DoS
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context, timeout=10) as response:
            content = response.read().decode('utf-8')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description
        desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
                              content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError, ValueError, socket.gaierror):
        # Return default values for all error cases
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from email.message import Message
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Validate and normalize URL
        parsed_url = urlparse(page_url)
        if parsed_url.scheme not in ('http', 'https'):
            # Try adding HTTPS scheme if missing
            page_url = f'https://{page_url}'
            parsed_url = urlparse(page_url)
            if parsed_url.scheme not in ('http', 'https') or not parsed_url.netloc:
                return {
                    'title': 'Content unavailable',
                    'description': 'Invalid URL: must be HTTP/HTTPS with a valid domain'
                }
        if not parsed_url.netloc:
            return {
                'title': 'Content unavailable',
                'description': 'Invalid URL: missing domain name'
            }

        # Fetch content with SSL context and timeout
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context, timeout=10) as response:
            # Get charset from Content-Type header or default to UTF-8
            content_type = response.getheader('Content-Type', '')
            msg = Message()
            msg['Content-Type'] = content_type
            charset = msg.get_content_charset() or 'utf-8'
            content = response.read().decode(charset, errors='replace')

        # Extract and normalize title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        if title_match:
            title_raw = title_match.group(1).strip()
            title = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', title_raw)
        else:
            title = "No title found"

        # Extract meta description (regular or Open Graph)
        description = None

        # First try regular meta description (any attribute order)
        meta_desc_tag = re.search(
            r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*>',
            content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL
        )
        if meta_desc_tag:
            content_match = re.search(
                r'content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
                meta_desc_tag.group(0), re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL
            )
            if content_match:
                desc_raw = content_match.group(1).strip()
                description = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', desc_raw)

        # Fallback to Open Graph description if regular is missing
        if not description:
            meta_og_tag = re.search(
                r'<meta[^>]*property=["\']og:description["\'][^>]*>',
                content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL
            )
            if meta_og_tag:
                content_match = re.search(
                    r'content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
                    meta_og_tag.group(0), re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL
                )
                if content_match:
                    desc_raw = content_match.group(1).strip()
                    description = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', desc_raw)

        # Set default description if none found
        if not description:
            description = "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except Exception as e:
        # Catch all exceptions to ensure consistent return format
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': f'Unable to fetch page content: {str(e)}'
        }

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi
import html


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Handle URLs without a scheme (e.g., "example.com" → "https://example.com")
        parsed_url = urlparse(page_url)
        if not parsed_url.scheme:
            page_url = f"https://{page_url}"

        # Fetch the content of the specified URL with proper SSL context
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context) as response:
            # Detect encoding from Content-Type header, fall back to UTF-8
            content_type = response.getheader('Content-Type', '')
            charset = 'utf-8'
            if 'charset=' in content_type:
                charset = content_type.split('charset=')[1].split(';')[0].strip()
            content = response.read().decode(charset, errors='replace')

        # Extract and clean title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        if title_match:
            title_raw = title_match.group(1).strip()
            title_normalized = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', title_raw)
            title = html.unescape(title_normalized)
        else:
            title = "No title found"

        # Extract and clean meta description (handles any attribute order)
        desc_match = re.search(
            r'<meta[^>]*?(?=[^>]*name=["\']description["\'])[^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
            content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL
        )
        if desc_match:
            desc_raw = desc_match.group(1).strip()
            desc_normalized = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', desc_raw)
            description = html.unescape(desc_normalized)
        else:
            description = "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError, ValueError):
        # Handle all common errors related to fetching or parsing the page
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Fetch the content of the specified URL with proper SSL context
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context) as response:
            content_bytes = response.read()
            # Get encoding from response headers or use UTF-8 as fallback
            encoding = response.headers.get_content_charset() or 'utf-8'
            try:
                content = content_bytes.decode(encoding)
            except UnicodeDecodeError:
                # Fall back to latin-1 which can decode any byte sequence
                content = content_bytes.decode('latin-1')

        # Extract and clean title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        if title_match:
            raw_title = title_match.group(1).strip()
            # Replace multiple whitespace (newlines, tabs, spaces) with single space
            title = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', raw_title) if raw_title else "No title found"
        else:
            title = "No title found"

        # Extract meta description with social media fallbacks
        desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
                              content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        if not desc_match:
            desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*property=["\']og:description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
                                  content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        if not desc_match:
            desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']twitter:description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
                                  content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)

        if desc_match:
            raw_desc = desc_match.group(1).strip()
            description = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', raw_desc) if raw_desc else "No description available"
        else:
            description = "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, ValueError):
        # Handle network errors, HTTP errors, and invalid URLs gracefully
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

"""
Module for scraping social media relevant metadata from web pages.
"""

import urllib.request
from urllib.error import URLError, HTTPError
import ssl
import certifi
from html.parser import HTMLParser


class MetaParser(HTMLParser):
    """HTML parser to extract title and meta description (including Open Graph) from web pages."""
    
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.title = ''
        self.description = ''
        self._in_title_tag = False

    def handle_starttag(self, tag: str, attrs: list[tuple[str, str | None]]) -> None:
        """Handle start tags to detect title and meta description elements."""
        tag_lower = tag.lower()
        
        if tag_lower == 'title':
            self._in_title_tag = True
        elif tag_lower == 'meta':
            # Convert attributes to lowercase keys for case-insensitive lookup
            attr_dict = {key.lower(): value.strip() if value else '' for key, value in attrs}
            
            # Check for standard meta description first
            if attr_dict.get('name') == 'description' and not self.description:
                self.description = attr_dict.get('content', '').strip()
            # Check for Open Graph description as fallback (relevant for social media)
            elif attr_dict.get('property') == 'og:description' and not self.description:
                self.description = attr_dict.get('content', '').strip()

    def handle_data(self, data: str) -> None:
        """Handle data inside tags to collect title content."""
        if self._in_title_tag:
            self.title += data

    def handle_endtag(self, tag: str) -> None:
        """Handle end tags to exit title tag tracking."""
        if tag.lower() == 'title':
            self._in_title_tag = False


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetch title and description metadata from a given webpage URL.
    
    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public webpage (e.g., 'https://example.com/article').
        
    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page title and description.
        Example: {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}.
        If metadata cannot be fetched or parsed, empty strings are returned for missing fields.
    """
    title = ''
    description = ''
    
    try:
        # Use a browser-like User-Agent to avoid being blocked by servers
        request = urllib.request.Request(
            page_url,
            headers={
                'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/120.0.0.0 Safari/537.36'
            }
        )
        
        # Configure SSL context with certifi's trusted CA bundle for secure HTTPS requests
        ssl_context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        
        # Fetch and process the webpage content
        with urllib.request.urlopen(request, context=ssl_context) as response:
            # Determine the correct character encoding from response headers
            content_type = response.headers.get('Content-Type', '')
            encoding = 'utf-8'
            if 'charset=' in content_type:
                encoding = content_type.split('charset=')[-1].strip()
            
            # Read and decode content, replacing invalid characters gracefully
            html_content = response.read().decode(encoding, errors='replace')
            
            # Parse HTML to extract metadata
            parser = MetaParser()
            parser.feed(html_content)
            
            # Clean up and assign results
            title = parser.title.strip()
            description = parser.description.strip()
            
    except (URLError, HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError, ssl.SSLError) as e:
        # Handle common errors (network issues, invalid responses, encoding problems)
        # In production, consider logging these errors for debugging
        pass
    
    return {'title': title, 'description': description}

def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Fetch the content of the specified URL with proper SSL context
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context) as response:
            # Get encoding from Content-Type header, default to UTF-8
            content_type = response.getheader('Content-Type')
            encoding = 'utf-8'
            if content_type:
                charset_match = re.search(r'charset=([^;]+)', content_type, re.IGNORECASE)
                if charset_match:
                    encoding = charset_match.group(1).strip()
            
            # Read and decode content with detected encoding
            content = response.read().decode(encoding)

        # Extract title (handles attributes in title tag)
        title_match = re.search(r'<title[^>]*>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)

            content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL
        )
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError, ValueError, TypeError) as e:
        # Return default values for any expected error
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }
    except Exception as e:
        # Catch-all for unexpected errors to maintain function contract
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'An unexpected error occurred while fetching content'
        }

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
import re
import ssl
import certifi
import html
from typing import Dict


def _clean_text(text: str) -> str:
    """Cleans text by removing HTML tags, unescaping entities, and normalizing whitespace."""
    # Remove HTML tags (including multi-line tags)
    text = re.sub(r'<.*?>', '', text, flags=re.DOTALL)
    # Unescape HTML entities (e.g., " -> ", & -> &)
    text = html.unescape(text)
    # Collapse multiple whitespace characters into a single space
    text = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', text)
    # Strip leading/trailing whitespace
    return text.strip()


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> Dict[str, str]:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Fetch the content of the specified URL with SSL context
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context) as response:
            content_bytes = response.read()
            
            # Determine the correct encoding from headers or meta tags
            charset = response.info().get_content_charset()
            if not charset:
                # Temporarily decode with latin1 (safe 1:1 byte-to-char mapping) to find meta tags
                temp_content = content_bytes.decode('latin1')
                # Check for <meta charset="...">
                charset_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*charset=["\'](.*?)["\']', temp_content, re.IGNORECASE)
                if charset_match:
                    charset = charset_match.group(1).strip()
                else:
                    # Check for <meta http-equiv="Content-Type" content="...; charset=...">
                    charset_match = re.search(
                        r'<meta[^>]*http-equiv=["\']Content-Type["\'][^>]*content=["\'].*?charset=(.*?)["\']',
                        temp_content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL
                    )
                    if charset_match:
                        charset = charset_match.group(1).strip()
                    else:
                        # Fallback to UTF-8 if no charset found
                        charset = 'utf-8'
            
            # Decode content with robust fallback
            try:
                content = content_bytes.decode(charset)
            except UnicodeDecodeError:
                # Use UTF-8 with replacement for invalid characters to avoid crashes
                content = content_bytes.decode('utf-8', errors='replace')

        # Extract and clean title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = _clean_text(title_match.group(1)) if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract primary description (name="description")
        desc_match = re.search(
            r'<meta(?=[^>]*name=["\']description["\'])[^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
            content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL
        )
        if desc_match:
            description = _clean_text(desc_match.group(1))
        else:
            # Fallback to Open Graph description (property="og:description")
            og_desc_match = re.search(
                r'<meta(?=[^>]*property=["\']og:description["\'])[^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
                content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL
            )
            if og_desc_match:
                description = _clean_text(og_desc_match.group(1))
            else:
                description = "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, ValueError, UnicodeDecodeError) as e:

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi
import ipaddress


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid HTTP/HTTPS URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Validate URL scheme and hostname
        parsed_url = urlparse(page_url)
        
        # Allow only HTTP/HTTPS schemes
        if parsed_url.scheme not in ('http', 'https'):
            raise ValueError("Only HTTP and HTTPS URLs are supported")
        
        hostname = parsed_url.hostname
        if not hostname:
            raise ValueError("Invalid URL: Missing hostname")
        
        # Block private/loopback IP addresses
        try:
            ip_addr = ipaddress.ip_address(hostname)
            if ip_addr.is_private or ip_addr.is_loopback:
                raise ValueError("Access to private/loopback addresses is restricted")
        except ValueError:
            # Hostname is not an IP address - skip IP check (add DNS resolution for full protection)
            pass

        # Fetch the content of the specified URL
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context, timeout=10) as response:
            # Handle different encodings from response headers
            content_type = response.headers.get('Content-Type', '')
            encoding = 'utf-8'
            if 'charset=' in content_type:
                encoding = content_type.split('charset=')[1].split(';')[0].strip()
            content = response.read().decode(encoding, errors='replace')

        # Extract title (robust to whitespace and newlines)
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description (handles single/double quotes and whitespace)
        desc_match = re.search(
            r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
            content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL
        )
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError, ValueError) as e:
        # Return user-friendly error information
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': f'Unable to fetch page content: {str(e)}'
        }

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Handle URLs without explicit scheme (add https:// by default)
        parsed_url = urlparse(page_url)
        if not parsed_url.scheme:
            page_url = f"https://{page_url}"
        
        # Fetch the content of the specified URL
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context) as response:
            raw_content = response.read()
            
            # Get encoding from Content-Type header, default to UTF-8
            content_type = response.getheader('Content-Type', '')
            encoding = 'utf-8'
            if 'charset=' in content_type:
                encoding_match = re.search(r'charset=([^;]+)', content_type, re.IGNORECASE)
                if encoding_match:
                    encoding = encoding_match.group(1).strip()
            
            # Decode content with fallback for invalid encodings
            try:
                content = raw_content.decode(encoding)
            except UnicodeDecodeError:
                content = raw_content.decode('utf-8', errors='replace')

        # Extract title (handles tags with attributes like lang="en")
        title_match = re.search(r'<title[^>]*>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract Open Graph description first (priority for social media)
        og_desc_match = re.search(
            r'<meta(?=[^>]*property=["\']og:description["\'])[^>]*?content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
            content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL
        )
        if og_desc_match:
            description = og_desc_match.group(1).strip()
        else:
            # Extract regular meta description (handles attribute order)
            desc_match = re.search(
                r'<meta(?=[^>]*name=["\']description["\'])[^>]*?content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
                content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL
            )
            description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError, ValueError, TypeError):
        # Gracefully handle all common errors and return fallback values
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.title_raw = []
        self.description = ''
        self.in_title = False

    def handle_starttag(self, tag, attrs):
        """Handle start tags to detect title and meta description elements."""
        attrs_dict = dict(attrs)
        tag_lower = tag.lower()
        
        if tag_lower == 'title':
            self.in_title = True
        elif tag_lower == 'meta':
            name_attr = attrs_dict.get('name', '').lower()
            if name_attr == 'description' and not self.description:

    def handle_data(self, data):
        """Collect text data inside the title tag."""
        if self.in_title:
            self.title_raw.append(data)

    def handle_endtag(self, tag):
        """Handle end tags to exit title tracking."""
        if tag.lower() == 'title':

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi
import socket
import ipaddress


def validate_url_for_ssrf(url: str) -> None:
    """Validates a URL to prevent SSRF attacks by checking against private/reserved IP ranges."""
    parsed_url = urlparse(url)
    
    # Only allow HTTP/HTTPS schemes
    if parsed_url.scheme not in ("http", "https"):
        raise ValueError(f"Unsupported URL scheme: {parsed_url.scheme}")
    
    hostname = parsed_url.hostname
    if not hostname:
        raise ValueError("URL does not contain a valid hostname")
    
    # Resolve hostname to IP addresses (handles both IPv4 and IPv6)
    try:
        addrinfo_list = socket.getaddrinfo(
            hostname,
            parsed_url.port or (443 if parsed_url.scheme == "https" else 80),
            socket.AF_UNSPEC,
            socket.SOCK_STREAM
        )
    except socket.gaierror as e:
        raise ValueError(f"Failed to resolve hostname: {hostname}") from e
    
    # Check each resolved IP address
    for addrinfo in addrinfo_list:
        ip_address = addrinfo[4][0]
        try:
            # Check IPv4 addresses
            ipv4 = ipaddress.IPv4Address(ip_address)
            if any([
                ipv4.is_private,
                ipv4.is_loopback,
                ipv4.is_link_local,
                ipv4.is_reserved,
                ipv4.is_unspecified
            ]):
                raise ValueError(f"Private or restricted IPv4 address detected: {ip_address}")
        except ipaddress.AddressValueError:
            # Check IPv6 addresses
            try:
                ipv6 = ipaddress.IPv6Address(ip_address)
                if any([
                    ipv6.is_private,
                    ipv6.is_loopback,
                    ipv6.is_link_local,
                    ipv6.is_reserved,
                    ipv6.is_unspecified,
                    ipv6.is_site_local
                ]):
                    raise ValueError(f"Private or restricted IPv6 address detected: {ip_address}")
            except ipaddress.AddressValueError:
                raise ValueError(f"Invalid IP address: {ip_address}")


class SSRFProtectRedirectHandler(urllib.request.HTTPRedirectHandler):
    def redirect_request(self, req, fp, code, msg, headers, newurl):
        """Override redirect handling to validate the new URL before following."""
        try:

        except ValueError as e:
            raise urllib.error.HTTPError(

                403,

                fp
